notes: KNOWN FAILING — phases t3_start/t3_end FAIL (6/8). INHERENT ProcessOwnedStore observability gap: opencode spawns a FRESH process per `opencode run`, so three chained --session turns are three short-lived processes against one ses_… SQLite row. The daemon's poller samples session state on its tick rather than streaming per-turn events, so the first run surfaces a clean working→ready→process_exited arc but the two subsequent back-to-back runs coalesce into a SINGLE observed working→ready arc — only 2 of 3 per-turn state arcs are observable. The LOAD-BEARING claim of token-accounting still passes: all three turns DID run and TotalTokens accumulated monotonically (transcript step-finish.tokens.total 21041→21057→21081, input 21039→21055→21079), captured by the parser's PerTurnContribution; phases t1_start/t1_end/t2_start/t2_end + session_birth + pid_bind all PASS. This is NOT recipe-fixable (the inter-turn sleeps are already ≥5s; tuning sleeps to separate two same-ses_ processes would be tuning to the recording, not the spec) and NOT a spec overspecification (three turns genuinely ran). Clears if the daemon gains per-turn (not per-process-poll) state observation for ProcessOwnedStore transports, or if opencode keeps one long-lived process across chained turns. The phase lines stay as the documented target. ORIGINAL RATIONALE: Token & cost extraction for a single turn is covered by basic-turn; this scenario stresses per-turn accumulation across MULTIPLE turns. OpenCode runs each turn headlessly via a blocking `opencode run` (the driver chains --session for continuity); session state lives in OpenCode's SQLite store, observed by the daemon's ProcessOwnedStore source as the UUID-keyed ses_… session. The phase DSL only asserts state arcs and event kinds (no TotalTokens / cache-bucket metric form), so the token-accounting evidence lives in events.jsonl: each step-finish emits a PerTurnContribution whose UsageBreakdown carries input/output and a nested cache object (cache.read → CacheRead, cache.write → CacheCreation5m), and SessionMetrics.TotalTokens accumulates monotonically. After turn 1 TotalTokens > 0; after turn 2 and turn 3 TotalTokens ≥ the previous total. The recording captures three turns so the monotonic-accumulation invariant is visible across two boundaries (T1→T2, T2→T3). session_birth is anchored to recording start and left UNPINNED so a transient proc-<PID> presession cannot steal identity and cascade same_session_as failures; t1_start (matching working) establishes the session identity that later phases chain off via same_session_as. The `no state_transition to waiting` invariant covers tool_calls_max:0 — three no-tool turns never gate on a permission ask.

text: Session appears in ready within 2s of agent launch; never enters waiting (no tool use)
PID bound to the ses_… session
Turn 1 user prompt drives the ses_… session ready → working
Turn 1 completes back to ready on the same ses_… session; first PerTurnContribution flushed, TotalTokens > 0
Turn 2 user prompt → working on the same ses_… session
Turn 2 completes back to ready; TotalTokens after T2 ≥ TotalTokens after T1 (monotonic)
Turn 3 user prompt → working on the same ses_… session
Turn 3 completes back to ready; TotalTokens after T3 ≥ TotalTokens after T2 (monotonic accumulation across 3 turns)
